title: streptanthus glandulosus subsp. albidus
intro: Streptanthus glandulosus subsp. albidus, also known as the white clematis or white lepidium, is a rare plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to California in the United States. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and fighting off infections, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Streptanthus glandulosus subsp. albidus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
